title: amauropelta globulifera
intro: Amauropelta globulifera, also known as the globe-leaved Amauropelta, is a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae. It is a type of vascular plant that is native to tropical regions and is known for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Amauropelta globulifera can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
